title: Panic First Aid: Breathe Psalm 46
script: When panic hits, you don’t need perfect faith—you need a rhythm. Breathe with me. Inhale 4 on 'Be still', hold 4 on 'and know', exhale 4 on 'that I am', hold 4 on 'God'. That’s one box. Do it three times. Now ground: name 3 things you see, 2 you can touch, 1 sound you hear. Your heart is racing because adrenaline surged—but waves crest and fall. You are safe in His refuge. Quick prayer: Lord, steady my breath and my mind. Save this as your plan: Psalm 46 box x3, 3-2-1 grounding, short prayer. Replay whenever you need.

title: Be Still Reset: 30-Second Panic Plan
hook: If you're feeling anxious, watch this.
script: Right now, put a hand on your belly. Breath 1: slow inhale through your nose; whisper, 'God is our refuge.' Exhale, 'and strength.' Breath 2: inhale, 'an ever-present help'; exhale, 'in trouble.' Breath 3: inhale calm, exhale long. Press your feet into the floor. Unclench your jaw. Look for one color around you and name it. Panic feels permanent, but it’s a false fire alarm—loud, not lethal. You are not alone; God is near. Prayer: Father, meet me in this breath. Screenshot this reset and keep it handy. Come back and breathe with me anytime.

title: Calm the Alarm with Psalm 46
hook: If God feels silent right now, watch this.
script: Your heart is pounding, hands shaky? That’s your body thinking there’s a lion. Let’s tell it the truth. Inhale 4 as you say, 'Be still.' Hold 7, 'and know that I am.' Exhale 8, 'God.' Repeat twice. Now orient: name your full name, today’s date, where you are. Touch something cool. Hear this promise from Psalm 46: 'God is our refuge and strength.' The surge will pass; you are safe in Him. Short prayer: Jesus, quiet the storm inside me. Safety card to remember: 4-7-8 with Psalm 46, orient name-date-place, pray one sentence. Save this and share it with someone who needs it.
